Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

John Taber papers, 1880-1965.
Collection Number: 2202

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
John Taber papers, 1880-1965.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
2202
Abstract:
Files include correspondence with constituents, colleagues, special interest groups and others; memoranda and statistical information; press releases and copies of speeches; printed House and Senate bills and committe reports; and pamphlets and clippings concerning congressional committees, legislation under consideration, and numerous items of public interest, both to the nation as a whole and to his constituency. Also correspondence, deed searches, and other papers, 1919-1946, of the law firm, Taber and Parker, and occasional personal papers of Representative Taber.
Creator:
Taber, John, 1880-1965.
Quanitities:
186.6 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Lawyer, legislator; U.S. Congressman from Auburn, New York (1923-1963).
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Files include correspondence with constituents, colleagues, special interest groups and others; memoranda and statistical information; press releases and copies of speeches; printed House and Senate bills and committe reports; and pamphlets and clippings concerning congressional committees, legislation under consideration, and numerous items of public interest, both to the nation as a whole and to his constituency. Also correspondence, deed searches, and other papers, 1919-1946, of the law firm, Taber and Parker, and occasional personal papers of Representative Taber.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

John Taber papers, #2202. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Taber and Parker (Firm)
United States. Congress. House
United States. Congress -- : Leadership.
United States. Congress. House -- : Public relations.
Places:
Auburn (N.Y.) -- Politics and government.
United States -- Politics and government -- 20th century.
Subjects:
Legislators -- United States.
